授業目的: 本授業の目的は、国際的に活躍する公認会計士(CPA)や公認管理会計士(CMA)、証券アナリスト、大手金融機関の管理職、グローバルな事業法人の財務管理者や会計･財務担当役員、CFO等の会計･財務プロフェッショナルを目指す者に不可欠な素養である英語でコーポレート･ファイナンス（財務管理／経営財務）をコミュニケートし使いこなすための基礎的な知識と技能を効率的かつ効果的に習得することである。
到達目標: 本授業は、公認会計士(CPA)や公認管理会計士(CMA)、証券アナリスト、大手金融機関の管理職、グローバルな事業法人の財務管理者や財務担当役員、CFO等の会計･財務プロフェッショナルを目指す学生が、ポートフォリオ理論や投資・資金調達・ペイアウトの意思決定に関する理論、企業価値を評価するためのアプローチ等のコーポレート･ファイナンス（財務管理／経営財務）の主要テーマを、限定された語彙による英語で説明･活用できるようになることを到達目標にしている。
関連科目: Students are expected to be familiar with introductory finance in Japanese and/or basic managerial accounting in English.
授業の概要・背景: For the aim of enabling students to engage in corporate financial management in English, this course provides a rigorous introduction to corporate finance/financial management with attention to challenges arising in the private sector, through a flexible class format.
授業方法: For the convenience of double-school students and part-time students who are full-time employees in the private/public sector, the 2026 class will be conducted as a "Hiflex" lesson so that the students can choose to attend either face-to-face or online.  The course employs lecture-discussions.  Several classes may be devoted to exercises.  Midterm "tale-home" exam and final "open-book/on-line" examination will be assigned.  Classes on day 4 & 5 will be held online so as to deal with the in-class examinations.

第1回:
Financial markets:
Securities market, Money market vs. Capital market, Primary market vs. Secondary market, Financial intermediaries, the Efficient Market Hypothesis (EMH)
Pre-enrollment preparation may take an average student approximately 4 - 5 hours.
第2回:
Interest rates:
Determinants of interest rates, Term structure of interest rates, Yield curves, Liquidity preference theory, Expectations theory, Market segmentation theory, Duration & Duration hedging
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第3回:
Risk measurement:
Definition of "Risk," Investment risk, Specific types of risk, Variance vs. Standard Deviation, Covariance, Effects of diversification
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第4回:
Portfolio management:
Expected return, Indifference curve, Efficient frontier vs. Optimal portfolio, Capital Market Line (CML), Variability (risk) of a portfolio's return, Company-specific risk vs.  Market risk
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第5回:
Capital Asset Pricing Model (CAPM):
Beta coefficient, Capital market equilibrium, Security Market Line (SML), Risk  premium, the required rate on equity capital, Dividend growth model, Arbitrage Pricing Theory (APT)
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第6回:
Long-term capital financing:
Sources of long-term financing, Types of stocks, Types of bonds, Intermediate-term financing, the Perfect Market, Propositions by Miller and Modigliani ( "MM" ), Financial structure vs. Capital structure
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第7回:
Optimal capitalization:
Cost of capital, Weighted-Average Cost of Capital (WACC), Opimal capital structure
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第8回:
Leverage:
Financial leverage and financial risk, Operating leverage and business risk, Measurement of total risk and the firm's cost of capital
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第9回:
Capital budgeting:
Marginal cost of capital, Marginal efficiency of investment, Equilibrium investment level, Capital budgeting techniques
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第10回:
Investment analysis:
Growth/expansion vs. Replacement, Independent projects, Mutually-exclusive projects, Decisions under capital rationing
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第11回:
Payout policy:
Dividend vs. Stock repurchase, Types of dividends, Signaling hypothesis, Clientele effect
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第12回:
Financial derivatives:
Hedge vs Speculation, Means of hedging, Futures contracts, Forward agreements, Swaps, Options
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第13回:
Valuation:
Investment analysis vs. Business valuation, Accounting model vs. Economic model, the Free Cash Flow (FCF) Approach, the Economic Value Added (EVA) Approach
Preparation and review may take an average student approximately 4 hours.
第14回:
Business combinations and Restructurings:
Types of business combinations, Mergers and Acquisitions (M&A), Reasons for business combinations, Valuation and pricing the combination, Market Value Added (MVA), Other restructurings
Comprehensive review may take an average student approximately 4 - 5 hours.
トピック.授業外学習2: Classes will be conducted on the assumption that students have read relevant portions of reference materials in advance.

教科書/Required texts0: Students will not be required to purchase a textbook for this course although the reference book mentioned below is recommended.  You may refer to any book whose content is applicable to the subject.
参考書/Reference books1: Brealey, Richard, Stewart Myers, Franklin Allen, and Alex Edmans
参考文献・資料/Reference books0: At the ouset of the class, PowerPoint resume carrying summary subject content will be provided.  Students should refer to each slide in relation to the upcoming exam topics as quiz and exam will be largely based on the lecture materials including th resume and the above-mentioned reference book.
学生による授業評価の方法Course Evaluation by Student: To be conducted in the last class by using a standard form designated by IBA.

各授業科目は、各学部・研究科の定めるディプロマ・ポリシー（DP）・カリキュラム・ポリシー（CP）に基づき、カリキュラム上に配置されています。DP・CPを意識して本授業科目の学修を進めることで、各学部・研究科の期待する能力が養成されます。各学部・研究科のDP、CPや教育課程表（授業科目一覧等）は、下記リンクこちらに掲載していますので学修計画の参考にしてください。https://kwic.kwansei.ac.jp/cabinet/reference?typeCd=0&cabinetId=557&directLink=1※kwicへのリンクとなります（在学生のみ閲覧可）
成績評価Grading0: 評価基準等　Grading Criteria etc.
成績評価Grading3: 備考　Note ・Attendance and class contribution account for 20% of the course grade, whereas a midterm "take-home" exam contributes toward 40% of the course grade.・ The mid-term examination will take place after the 4th day. ・ Final "open-book/on-line" examination to be held in the 8th week carries the remaining 40% credit.・ The midterm exam comprises numerical calculation and writing task, and the final examination includes multiple-choice questions and essay-type questions.・ Evaluation will be conducted in accordance with the relative assessment criteria.
備考Note0: Please bring a calculator. (PCs and smartphones are not allowed.)

name: 【550】ファイナンス英語講義／Principles of Corporate Finance

・Attendance and class contribution account for 20% of the course grade, whereas a midterm "take-home" exam contributes toward 40% of the course grade.

・ Final "open-book/on-line" examination to be held in the 8th week carries the remaining 40% credit.
